abstract.t: This document defines the Vanta audit receipt format, receipt chaining model, event classes, and verification rules for cryptographically verifiable execution tracing.

section.name: Overview
section.t: Vanta includes a protocol-native audit layer for important protocol and application events. Audit receipts are intended to be compact, deterministic, signed, and tamper-evident.

section.name: Audit Event Classes
section.t: Audit receipts may be emitted for mutation application, duplicate handling, validation failures, retransmissions, and session lifecycle events.

section.name: Receipt Format
section.t: An audit receipt contains at least a receipt identifier, a previous receipt hash, session and stream identifiers, message and operation identifiers where applicable, sender and receiver peer identifiers, an event type, a disposition code, a payload hash, a timestamp, and a receiver signature.

section.name: Payload Hashing
section.t: Audit records MUST NOT include plaintext payload content. Instead, they bind to payload bytes using a cryptographic hash. For end-to-end encrypted payloads, the ciphertext SHOULD be hashed for audit binding.

section.name: Receipt Chain Construction
section.t: Audit receipts form an append-only hash chain. Each receipt commits to the previous receipt hash.

section.name: Receipt Signatures
section.t: Audit receipts are signed by the receiver using its long-term Ed25519 identity key. This provides authenticity, attribution, and tamper evidence.

section.name: Verification
section.t: A verifier checks the receipt signature, recomputes the receipt hash, verifies continuity to the previous receipt hash, and validates the payload hash and referenced identifiers where available.

section.name: Cross-Session Continuity
section.t: Implementations MAY support cross-session audit continuity by referencing the terminal receipt hash of a previous session.

section.name: Privacy Properties
section.t: The audit layer intentionally stores metadata and hashes rather than payload contents or full application queries. This is intended to balance verifiability with confidentiality.

section.name: Audit Failure Handling
section.t: If receipt generation or verification fails under a policy that requires audit integrity, the implementation SHOULD emit an explicit protocol error and MAY terminate the session.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: Compromise of a peer's long-term signing key can invalidate receipt attribution for that peer. Implementations SHOULD protect long-term keys and receipt logs accordingly.
